answer:
Verdict: out. Team of 4, standard subscriptions + usage — event sourcing wrong tool here.

Why out:
- Billing already has natural "event log": invoices, ledger entries, payment records. Append-only ledger table gives you audit trail without event-sourcing machinery.
- Usage charges = metering problem, not event-sourcing problem. Ingest usage events into table/queue, aggregate at invoice time. That's just data pipeline — no CQRS, projections, replay needed.
- Real costs hit small team hard: event schema versioning, upcasting old events, projection rebuilds, eventual consistency bugs, snapshotting, debugging "why is balance wrong" across replayed streams. Easily 2-3x build time, permanent ops tax.
- Billing needs corrections (refunds, backdated plan changes, disputed usage). Event sourcing makes retroactive fixes painful — must emit compensating events, re-derive state. Mutable ledger with adjustment entries simpler and auditors accept it fine.
- Excitement is the tell. "Excited to build with X" = résumé-driven design. Customers want correct invoices, not architecture.

What to build instead:
- Postgres. Double-entry ledger table (append-only, immutable rows) — gets 90% of event-sourcing benefit: audit, replay-ability of balances.
- Usage events table, idempotency keys on ingest, periodic aggregation job.
- Subscription state machine as plain rows + audit log.
- Stripe Billing / Lago / Metronome first — seriously consider before building at all. Buy unless billing IS your product differentiator.

When event sourcing would be right: many teams, complex temporal queries as core feature, regulator demands full state reconstruction, or write throughput demanding CQRS. Not you at 4 people.

Talked out. Ledger, not event store.
